Practical guide: how to create "handwritten" study notes with Google Gemini in seconds
those aesthetic hand-drawn notes you see everywhere?
most people spend hours on an iPad with a stylus trying to recreate them
but if you don't have the time or the artistic skills... you can skip all of that
here's exactly how to generate professional "handwritten" infographics using Google Gemini
takes maybe 60 seconds once you know the process
step 1: feed Gemini the right info
go to Google Gemini and give it the content you want visualized
you can just type a topic but honestly... uploading a PDF works way better
that way your final notes include the exact details you actually need
use this prompt to prep your content:
"Summarize the essential pillars of [topic] tailored specifically for [audience]. Maintain the original title and provide deep, explanatory detail using fluid, complete sentences. For every key concept, include a clear definition and describe a specific visual or diagram that would help illustrate that point. Avoid including any 'significance' or 'why it matters' sections, and keep all sub-headings as brief as possible."
step 2: turn text into art
once Gemini gives you the text summary, stay in the same conversation
now you ask it to visualize everything as a physical notebook page
run this prompt:
"Generate an educational visual on weathered, ruled paper inside a spiral notebook with a [9:16] aspect ratio. The art style should blend realistic colored pencil sketches with soft watercolor accents. Use clean, hand-printed drafting-style lettering for all text. At the top, place a stylized header banner. Directly below, feature the primary definition followed by a wide, cinematic illustration that spans the page. The middle section should contain a list of core ideas marked with custom icons. The bottom half of the page should be organized into a tiled arrangement of bordered boxes, each containing a labeled comparative sketch or classification diagram with its own brief caption. Do not include any titles except for the main header."
step 3: quick refinements
AI won't nail it perfectly every time
maybe there's a spelling error or a section looks off
don't start over from scratch
just tell Gemini exactly what to fix and it'll adjust the image for you
one small tip: if you want a horizontal layout for your laptop instead of vertical for your phone, swap the aspect ratio from 9:16 to 16:9
that's it
two prompts and you've got study notes that look like you spent hours drawing them I post tutorials like this regularly but the best stuff goes to my newsletter first
things like:
  • new AI News & tools before everyone's talking about them.
  • prompts I'm actually using.
  • research paper breakdowns in plain English.
